These need to be comprehensive and self-contained:","id":null,"provider_name":null,"provider_details":null,"part_kind":"text"},{"tool_name":"create_or_rewrite_file","args":"{\"target_file\": \"/home/workspace/N5/builds/frontend-skill/drops/D1.1-core-skill.md\", \"content\": \"---\\nbuild_slug: frontend-skill\\ndrop_id: D1.1\\nname: Core SKILL.md creation\\ntype: skill_build\\ncreated: 2026-02-06\\n---\\n\\n# D1.1: Core Frontend Design SKILL.md\\n\\n## Context\\n\\nWe're building a frontend design skill that generates high-quality landing pages. This is inspired by Anthropic's `frontend-design` skill which Theo (YouTube creator) praised for making Opus 4.5 dramatically better at design.\\n\\n**Key insight from research:** The skill's power comes from:\\n1. Anti-slop guardrails (avoiding purple gradients, generic AI aesthetics)\\n2. Multi-variation generation (create 5, pick best)\\n3. Clear platform-specific output instructions\\n\\n## MUST DO\\n\\n1. **Create `Skills/frontend/SKILL.md`** with proper Agent Skills frontmatter:\\n   - `name: frontend` (must match directory)\\n   - `description`: Clear description of what the skill does\\n   - `compatibility: Created for Zo Computer`\\n   - `metadata.author: va.zo.computer`\\n\\n2. **Input Spectrum Section** — The skill accepts varying degrees of input:\\n   - **Vibes only**: \\\"a modern SaaS landing page that feels trustworthy\\\"\\n   - **Colors/mood**: Hex values, mood words, reference images\\n   - **Content provided**: Headlines, copy, features list, CTAs\\n   - **Template specified**: Use a captured template from `templates/`\\n\\n3. **Generation Workflow Section**:\\n   - If input is sparse → ask clarifying questions OR make tasteful defaults\\n   - Always ask for target platform (or detect from context)\\n   - When `--variants N` is requested, generate N distinct directions\\n   - Apply anti-slop guardrails (reference `references/anti-patterns.md`)\\n\\n4. **Platform-Specific Output Sections** — Three platforms:\\n\\n   **Zo.space** (React/Tailwind/Hono):\\n   - Use `update_space_route(path, route_type=\\\"page\\\", code, public=True)`\\n   - Component must be default export React function\\n   - Use Tailwind 4 classes\\n   - Can import from `lucide-react` for icons\\n   - Reference any uploaded assets via their asset_path\\n\\n   **Standalone HTML**:\\n   - Single HTML file with Tailwind CDN (`<script src=\\\"https://cdn.tailwindcss.com\\\">`)\\n   - Inline all styles, no external dependencies\\n   - Should work when opened directly in browser\\n\\n   **Next.js** (App Router):\\n   - Export default async function component\\n   - Use `\\\"use client\\\"` directive if needed for interactivity\\n   - Tailwind classes, standard Next.js patterns\\n\\n5. **Anti-Slop Section** — Reference the anti-patterns file:\\n   ```markdown\\n   Before generating, review `references/anti-patterns.md` and ensure your design avoids:\\n   - Generic purple/blue gradients\\n   - Overly symmetrical layouts\\n   - Stock illustration aesthetic\\n   - Excessive animations\\n   - Poor typography hierarchy\\n   ```\\n\\n6. **Template Application Section** — When a template is provided:\\n   - Load the template YAML from `templates/<name>.yaml`\\n   - Apply palette, typography, layout values\\n   - Use `style_notes` for aesthetic guidance\\n   - Content still comes from user input\\n\\n## MUST NOT DO\\n\\n- Do NOT create any templates yet (that's Wave 2)\\n- Do NOT create the anti-patterns.md file (that's D1.2)\\n- Do NOT add complex scripting (keep it as a prompt/instructions skill)\\n- Do NOT hardcode specific design opinions beyond anti-slop — the skill should be flexible\\n\\n## Scope\\n\\n**Files to create:**\\n- `Skills/frontend/SKILL.md`\\n\\n**Files NOT to touch:**\\n- `Skills/frontend/templates/*` (Wave 2)\\n- `Skills/frontend/references/*` (D1.2)\\n\\n## Reference Material\\n\\n**Anthropic's frontend-design skill structure** (what works):\\n- Clear sections for different concerns\\n- Specific anti-patterns listed\\n- Platform-aware output instructions\\n- Emphasis on typography and whitespace\\n\\n**Vercel's web-design-guidelines** (complementary):\\n- Accessibility requirements\\n- Semantic HTML\\n- Performance considerations\\n\\n## Expected Output\\n\\nA complete `Skills/frontend/SKILL.md` that:\\n1. Has valid Agent Skills frontmatter\\n2. Explains the input spectrum clearly\\n3. Has platform-specific generation instructions for all 3 platforms\\n4. References the anti-patterns file (even though it doesn't exist yet)\\n5. Includes template application section (even though templates don't exist yet)\\n6. Is comprehensive enough that an AI reading it can generate quality landing pages\\n\\n## Deposit Location\\n\\nWrite your deposit to: `N5/builds/frontend-skill/deposits/D1.1.json`\\n\\n```json\\n{\\n  \\\"drop_id\\\": \\\"D1.1\\\",\\n  \\\"status\\\": \\\"complete\\\",\\n  \\\"files_created\\\": [\\\"Skills/frontend/SKILL.md\\\"],\\n  \\\"files_modified\\\": [],\\n  \\\"summary\\\": \\\"Brief description of what was created\\\",\\n  \\\"notes\\\": \\\"Any observations or decisions made\\\",\\n  \\\"ready_for_next\\\": true\\n}\\n```\\n\"}","tool_call_id":"toolu_01NibfNm6z9xBB78PgE8Tjkp","id":null,"provider_name":null,"provider_details":null,"part_kind":"tool-call"},{"tool_name":"create_or_rewrite_file","args":"{\"target_file\": \"/home/workspace/N5/builds/frontend-skill/drops/D1.2-anti-patterns.md\", \"content\": \"---\\nbuild_slug: frontend-skill\\ndrop_id: D1.2\\nname: Anti-patterns reference\\ntype: skill_build\\ncreated: 2026-02-06\\n---\\n\\n# D1.2: Anti-Patterns Reference Document\\n\\n## Context\\n\\nWe're building a frontend design skill. A key differentiator from generic AI design is explicit guardrails against \\\"AI slop\\\" — the clichéd, generic aesthetic that makes AI-generated designs immediately recognizable (in a bad way).\\n\\nThis reference document will be used by the main SKILL.md to ensure generated designs avoid common pitfalls.\\n\\n## MUST DO\\n\\n1. **Create `Skills/frontend/references/anti-patterns.md`** — A comprehensive catalog of what to avoid\\n\\n2. **Structure the document** with these sections:\\n\\n   **Color Anti-Patterns:**\\n   - Purple-to-blue gradients (the #1 AI slop signal)\\n   - Overly saturated neon accents\\n   - Too many colors (more than 3-4)\\n   - Generic \\\"tech\\\" palettes (blue + gray)\\n   - Gradients used as a crutch instead of intentional design\\n\\n   **Layout Anti-Patterns:**\\n   - Perfect symmetry everywhere (feels robotic)\\n   - Generic 3-column feature grids\\n   - No visual hierarchy (everything same weight)\\n   - Excessive whitespace OR cramped spacing\\n   - Predictable hero → features → testimonials → CTA flow without variation\\n\\n   **Typography Anti-Patterns:**\\n   - Too many font families (stick to 1-2)\\n   - Poor contrast (gray text on white)\\n   - No clear heading hierarchy\\n   - Overly stylized display fonts for body text\\n   - Inconsistent sizing scale\\n\\n   **Imagery Anti-Patterns:**\\n   - Generic isometric illustrations (the blob people)\\n   - Stock photos of diverse people pointing at screens\\n   - Overly polished 3D renders\\n   - Abstract gradients as hero backgrounds\\n   - AI-generated images (often have telltale artifacts)\\n\\n   **Animation Anti-Patterns:**\\n   - Everything animates on scroll\\n   - Parallax effects everywhere\\n   - Bouncing elements\\n   - Slow fade-ins that delay content visibility\\n   - Animation for animation's sake\\n\\n   **Copy Anti-Patterns:**\\n   - \\\"Revolutionize your workflow\\\"\\n   - \\\"Seamless integration\\\"\\n   - Buzzword soup\\n   - Overly long hero headlines\\n   - Generic CTAs (\\\"Get Started\\\", \\\"Learn More\\\")\\n\\n3. **For each anti-pattern, provide:**\\n   - What it looks like (specific description)\\n   - Why it's bad (the underlying problem)\\n   - What to do instead (the fix)\\n\\n4. **Add a \\\"Taste Test\\\" section** — Quick checklist:\\n   - [ ] Would this design look at home on a 2019 Dribbble shot? (If yes, reconsider)\\n   - [ ] Does it have a purple gradient? (Avoid unless specifically requested)\\n   - [ ] Are there more than 2 fonts? (Usually too many)\\n   - [ ] Does every section animate? (Probably too much)\\n   - [ ] Could you swap the content for any other SaaS and it still work? (Too generic)\\n\\n## MUST NOT DO\\n\\n- Do NOT include positive examples (that's what templates are for)\\n- Do NOT be preachy or condescending — this is a reference, not a lecture\\n- Do NOT make it so restrictive that all creativity is blocked\\n- Do NOT touch any other files\\n\\n## Scope\\n\\n**Files to create:**\\n- `Skills/frontend/references/anti-patterns.md`\\n\\n**Files NOT to touch:**\\n- `Skills/frontend/SKILL.md` (D1.1's job)\\n- `Skills/frontend/templates/*` (Wave 2)\\n\\n## Expected Output\\n\\nA comprehensive anti-patterns reference that:\\n1. Catalogs specific, recognizable AI design clichés\\n2. Explains why each is problematic\\n3. Provides alternatives\\n4. Is scannable and usable as a quick reference\\n5. Has a taste-test checklist for self-review\\n\\n## Deposit Location\\n\\nWrite your deposit to: `N5/builds/frontend-skill/deposits/D1.2.json`\\n\\n```json\\n{\\n  \\\"drop_id\\\": \\\"D1.2\\\",\\n  \\\"status\\\": \\\"complete\\\",\\n  \\\"files_created\\\": [\\\"Skills/frontend/references/anti-patterns.md\\\"],\\n  \\\"files_modified\\\": [],\\n  \\\"summary\\\": \\\"Brief description of what was created\\\",\\n  \\\"notes\\\": \\\"Any observations or decisions made\\\",\\n  \\\"ready_for_next\\\": true\\n}\\n```\\n\"}","tool_call_id":"toolu_01CZaBEwhL7kRYvSE2CMg6LM","id":null,"provider_name":null,"provider_details":null,"part_kind":"tool-call"},{"tool_name":"create_or_rewrite_file","args":"{\"target_file\": \"/home/workspace/N5/builds/frontend-skill/drops/D2.1-template-schema.md\", \"content\": \"---\\nbuild_slug: frontend-skill\\ndrop_id: D2.1\\nname: Template schema design\\ntype: skill_build\\ncreated: 2026-02-06\\ndepends_on: [D1.1]\\n---\\n\\n# D2.1: Template Schema Design\\n\\n## Context\\n\\nThe template system lets us capture \\\"design DNA\\\" from successful pages and reliably reproduce that aesthetic with different content. This is the schema that defines what a template contains.\\n\\n**Key design tension:** Templates need to be specific enough to produce consistent results, but flexible enough to work with different content.\\n\\n## MUST DO\\n\\n1. **Create `Skills/frontend/templates/_TEMPLATE.yaml`** — The format specification\\n\\n2. **Schema must include:**\\n\\n   ```yaml\\n   # Required metadata\\n   name: template-slug  # lowercase, hyphens only\\n   description: |\\n     What this template is good for. When to use it.\\n   source: |\\n     URL of the original page, or description of where this came from.\\n   created: YYYY-MM-DD\\n   \\n   # Color palette (all required)\\n   palette:\\n     primary: \\\"#hex\\\"      # Main brand color\\n     secondary: \\\"#hex\\\"    # Supporting color\\n     accent: \\\"#hex\\\"       # Highlights, CTAs\\n     background: \\\"#hex\\\"   # Page background\\n     surface: \\\"#hex\\\"      # Card/section backgrounds\\n     text: \\\"#hex\\\"         # Primary text\\n     text_muted: \\\"#hex\\\"   # Secondary text\\n   \\n   # Typography\\n   typography:\\n     heading_font: \\\"Font Name\\\"  # Google Fonts or system font\\n     body_font: \\\"Font Name\\\"\\n     mono_font: \\\"Font Name\\\"     # For code, optional\\n     scale: tight | normal | loose  # Overall spacing\\n     heading_weight: 400-900    # Font weight for headings\\n   \\n   # Layout\\n   layout:\\n     max_width: narrow | standard | wide | full\\n     # narrow: 768px, standard: 1024px, wide: 1280px, full: 100%\\n     spacing: compact | balanced | airy\\n     # compact: 1-2rem sections, balanced: 3-4rem, airy: 5-6rem\\n     border_radius: none | subtle | rounded | pill\\n     # none: 0, subtle: 4px, rounded: 8-12px, pill: 9999px\\n   \\n   # Sections (ordered list of what the page contains)\\n   sections:\\n     - type: hero | features | testimonials | pricing | cta | faq | stats | team | logos\\n       variant: string  # Optional variant name for the section type\\n   \\n   # Style notes (freeform, critical for capturing nuance)\\n   style_notes: |\\n     Freeform description of:\\n     - The overall aesthetic feel\\n     - Specific techniques used (shadows, borders, gradients)\\n     - What makes this template distinctive\\n     - Things that are hard to capture in structured fields\\n   \\n   # Optional: Reference images\\n   reference_images:\\n     - path: /path/to/image.png\\n       description: What this shows\\n   ```\\n\\n3. **Add documentation within the file** explaining:\\n   - Each field's purpose\\n   - Valid values for enum fields\\n   - How the template will be applied during generation\\n\\n4. **Design for extensibility** — Include a `custom` field for template-specific values:\\n   ```yaml\\n   custom:\\n     key: value  # Template-specific overrides\\n   ```\\n\\n## MUST NOT DO\\n\\n- Do NOT create actual templates (that's D2.2)\\n- Do NOT modify SKILL.md (that's D2.2's responsibility to add capture instructions)\\n- Do NOT over-engineer — keep it minimal but complete\\n\\n## Scope\\n\\n**Files to create:**\\n- `Skills/frontend/templates/_TEMPLATE.yaml`\\n\\n**Files NOT to touch:**\\n- `Skills/frontend/SKILL.md`\\n- `Skills/frontend/references/*`\\n\\n## Key Design Decisions\\n\\nDocument these in your deposit notes:\\n\\n1. **Why these specific palette colors?** (primary, secondary, accent, background, surface, text, text_muted)\\n2. **Why these layout options?** (the enum values chosen)\\n3. **What belongs in `style_notes` vs structured fields?**\\n\\n## Expected Output\\n\\nA `_TEMPLATE.yaml` that:\\n1. Serves as both schema definition AND documentation\\n2. Has clear comments explaining each field\\n3. Uses YAML anchors or comments to show valid values\\n4. Is complete enough that someone could create a template by filling it in\\n5. Balances structure with flexibility (style_notes for nuance)\\n\\n## Deposit Location\\n\\nWrite your deposit to: `N5/builds/frontend-skill/deposits/D2.1.json`\\n\\n```json\\n{\\n  \\\"drop_id\\\": \\\"D2.1\\\",\\n  \\\"status\\\": \\\"complete\\\",\\n  \\\"files_created\\\": [\\\"Skills/frontend/templates/_TEMPLATE.yaml\\\"],\\n  \\\"files_modified\\\": [],\\n  \\\"summary\\\": \\\"Brief description of what was created\\\",\\n  \\\"notes\\\": \\\"Design decisions made and rationale\\\",\\n  \\\"ready_for_next\\\": true\\n}\\n```\\n\"}","tool_call_id":"toolu_01GjkP11AQtxhFQjUawz6yDs","id":null,"provider_name":null,"provider_details":null,"part_kind":"tool-call"},{"tool_name":"create_or_rewrite_file","args":"{\"target_file\": \"/home/workspace/N5/builds/frontend-skill/drops/D2.2-seed-template.md\", \"content\": \"---\\nbuild_slug: frontend-skill\\ndrop_id: D2.2\\nname: Seed template + capture instructions\\ntype: skill_build\\ncreated: 2026-02-06\\ndepends_on: [D1.1, D2.1]\\n---\\n\\n# D2.2: Seed Template + Capture Instructions\\n\\n## Context\\n\\nWe need:\\n1. At least one working template to prove the system works\\n2. Instructions in SKILL.md for how to capture new templates from existing pages\\n\\n## MUST DO\\n\\n### Part 1: Seed Template\\n\\n1. **Create `Skills/frontend/templates/minimal-saas.yaml`** — A clean SaaS landing page template\\n\\n   Base it on good SaaS landing page patterns:\\n   - Clean, modern aesthetic\\n   - NOT the purple gradient AI slop\\n   - Good typography hierarchy\\n   - Restrained color palette\\n   - Professional but not boring\\n\\n   Example direction:\\n   ```yaml\\n   name: minimal-saas\\n   description: |\\n     Clean, professional SaaS landing page. Works for B2B tools,\\n     developer products, productivity apps. Emphasizes clarity\\n     and trust over flashiness.\\n   source: Synthesized from Linear, Vercel, Stripe landing page patterns\\n   created: 2026-02-06\\n   \\n   palette:\\n     primary: \\\"#0A0A0A\\\"      # Near-black\\n     secondary: \\\"#525252\\\"    # Gray\\n     accent: \\\"#2563EB\\\"       # Blue (restrained)\\n     background: \\\"#FFFFFF\\\"   # White\\n     surface: \\\"#FAFAFA\\\"      # Off-white\\n     text: \\\"#0A0A0A\\\"         # Near-black\\n     text_muted: \\\"#737373\\\"   # Gray\\n   \\n   typography:\\n     heading_font: \\\"Inter\\\"\\n     body_font: \\\"Inter\\\"\\n     scale: normal\\n     heading_weight: 600\\n   \\n   layout:\\n     max_width: standard\\n     spacing: balanced\\n     border_radius: subtle\\n   \\n   sections:\\n     - type: hero\\n       variant: centered\\n     - type: logos\\n       variant: simple\\n     - type: features\\n       variant: grid-3\\n     - type: testimonials\\n       variant: single-featured\\n     - type: cta\\n       variant: centered\\n   \\n   style_notes: |\\n     - Relies on typography and spacing, not color\\n     - Subtle shadows (shadow-sm) for depth\\n     - No gradients — solid colors only\\n     - Icons: Lucide, stroke-width 1.5\\n     - Buttons: solid fill, subtle hover state\\n     - Links: underline on hover, not color change\\n     - Overall feel: \\\"grown-up startup\\\"\\n   ```\\n\\n### Part 2: Capture Instructions\\n\\n2. **Update `Skills/frontend/SKILL.md`** — Add a \\\"Template Capture\\\" section:\\n\\n   The section should explain:\\n   \\n   a. **When to capture**: After generating a page the user loves\\n   \\n   b. **How to analyze**: Look at the page and extract:\\n      - Color palette (use browser devtools or eyedropper)\\n      - Typography (fonts, weights, scale)\\n      - Layout patterns (max-width, spacing, border-radius)\\n      - Section structure\\n      - The intangible \\\"feel\\\" (goes in style_notes)\\n   \\n   c. **How to create the template**:\\n      - Copy `templates/_TEMPLATE.yaml` as starting point\\n      - Fill in all fields based on analysis\\n      - Write detailed style_notes capturing nuance\\n      - Save as `templates/<name>.yaml`\\n   \\n   d. **Template naming conventions**:\\n      - Use descriptive slugs: `minimal-saas`, `bold-agency`, `warm-nonprofit`\\n      - Name should convey the aesthetic\\n\\n## MUST NOT DO\\n\\n- Do NOT modify the template schema (_TEMPLATE.yaml) — that's locked from D2.1\\n- Do NOT modify anti-patterns.md\\n- Do NOT create multiple templates — just the one seed template\\n\\n## Scope\\n\\n**Files to create:**\\n- `Skills/frontend/templates/minimal-saas.yaml`\\n\\n**Files to modify:**\\n- `Skills/frontend/SKILL.md` (add Template Capture section)\\n\\n**Files NOT to touch:**\\n- `Skills/frontend/templates/_TEMPLATE.yaml`\\n- `Skills/frontend/references/*`\\n\\n## Reading Required\\n\\nBefore starting, read:\\n- `Skills/frontend/templates/_TEMPLATE.yaml` (from D2.1) — understand the schema\\n- `Skills/frontend/SKILL.md` (from D1.1) — understand where to add capture section\\n\\n## Expected Output\\n\\n1. A working `minimal-saas.yaml` template that follows the schema exactly\\n2. A \\\"Template Capture\\\" section added to SKILL.md with clear instructions\\n3. The template should be good enough that generating a page from it produces something professional\\n\\n## Deposit Location\\n\\nWrite your deposit to: `N5/builds/frontend-skill/deposits/D2.2.json`\\n\\n```json\\n{\\n  \\\"drop_id\\\": \\\"D2.2\\\",\\n  \\\"status\\\": \\\"complete\\\",\\n  \\\"files_created\\\": [\\\"Skills/frontend/templates/minimal-saas.yaml\\\"],\\n  \\\"files_modified\\\": [\\\"Skills/frontend/SKILL.md\\\"],\\n  \\\"summary\\\": \\\"Brief description of what was created\\\",\\n  \\\"notes\\\": \\\"Any observations or decisions made\\\",\\n  \\\"ready_for_next\\\": true\\n}\\n```\\n\"}","tool_call_id":"toolu_011Ahqdv7BttUg69Ns6mVD2h","id":null,"provider_name":null,"provider_details":null,"part_kind":"tool-call"},{"tool_name":"create_or_rewrite_file","args":"{\"target_file\": \"/home/workspace/N5/builds/frontend-skill/drops/D3.1-validation.md\", \"content\": \"---\\nbuild_slug: frontend-skill\\ndrop_id: D3.1\\nname: Validation tests\\ntype: validation\\nspawn_mode: manual\\ncreated: 2026-02-06\\ndepends_on: [D2.1, D2.2]\\n---\\n\\n# D3.1: Validation Tests\\n\\n## Context\\n\\nThe skill and template system are built. Now we validate that everything works together.\\n\\n**This is a MANUAL drop** because validation requires judgment and V may want to see the outputs.\\n\\n## MUST DO\\n\\nRun these validation tests and document results:\\n\\n### Test 1: Vibe-Only Generation (Zo.space)\\n\\n**Input:** \\\"Create a landing page for a consulting firm that helps startups with go-to-market strategy. Should feel trustworthy and premium but not stuffy.\\\"\\n\\n**Expected:** \\n- Skill should ask clarifying questions OR make tasteful defaults\\n- Output should be valid Zo.space React/Tailwind code\\n- Design should avoid anti-patterns (no purple gradients, etc.)\\n\\n**How to test:**\\n1. Invoke the skill with the input above\\n2. Review the generated code\\n3. Optionally deploy to va.zo.space to see it live\\n\\n### Test 2: Template-Based Generation (Standalone HTML)\\n\\n**Input:** \\\"Create a landing page using the minimal-saas template. Content: A project management tool called 'Cadence' that helps remote teams stay synchronized. Target: standalone HTML.\\\"\\n\\n**Expected:**\\n- Should load and apply minimal-saas.yaml template\\n- Colors, typography, layout should match template\\n- Output should be single HTML file with Tailwind CDN\\n- Should work when opened in browser\\n\\n**How to test:**\\n1. Invoke skill with template specified\\n2. Save output as .html file\\n3. Open in browser and verify appearance\\n\\n### Test 3: Template Capture\\n\\n**Input:** After Test 1 or 2 produces a design you like, ask to capture it as a template.\\n\\n**Expected:**\\n- Skill should analyze the generated page\\n- Extract palette, typography, layout\\n- Produce valid YAML following _TEMPLATE.yaml schema\\n- style_notes should capture the nuance\\n\\n**How to test:**\\n1. Generate a page you like\\n2. Ask skill to capture it as a template\\n3. Verify output YAML is valid and complete\\n\\n### Test 4: Multi-Variant Generation\\n\\n**Input:** \\\"Create 3 variants of a landing page for a meditation app called 'Stillness'. Each should have a distinct aesthetic direction.\\\"\\n\\n**Expected:**\\n- 3 meaningfully different designs\\n- Not just color swaps — different layouts, typography, feels\\n- All avoid anti-patterns\\n\\n**How to test:**\\n1. Invoke skill with --variants 3 (or however it's specified)\\n2. Compare the 3 outputs\\n3. Verify they're genuinely different\\n\\n## Validation Rubric\\n\\nFor each test, score:\\n- **Functional**: Does the output work? (code runs, HTML renders)\\n- **Aesthetic**: Does it avoid AI slop? Would a human designer approve?\\n- **Faithful**: Does it follow the skill's instructions / template?\\n\\n## MUST NOT DO\\n\\n- Do NOT modify any skill files based on test results (just document)\\n- Do NOT skip any tests\\n\\n## Expected Output\\n\\nA detailed validation report documenting:\\n1. Each test run with actual input/output\\n2. Screenshots or descriptions of results\\n3. Pass/fail for each rubric dimension\\n4. Any issues discovered (for future improvement)\\n\\n## Deposit Location\\n\\nWrite your deposit to: `N5/builds/frontend-skill/deposits/D3.1.json`\\n\\n```json\\n{\\n  \\\"drop_id\\\": \\\"D3.1\\\",\\n  \\\"status\\\": \\\"complete\\\",\\n  \\\"files_created\\\": [],\\n  \\\"files_modified\\\": [],\\n  \\\"summary\\\": \\\"Validation results summary\\\",\\n  \\\"validation_results\\\": {\\n    \\\"test_1\\\": {\\\"functional\\\": true, \\\"aesthetic\\\": true, \\\"faithful\\\": true, \\\"notes\\\": \\\"...\\\"},\\n    \\\"test_2\\\": {\\\"functional\\\": true, \\\"aesthetic\\\": true, \\\"faithful\\\": true, \\\"notes\\\": \\\"...\\\"},\\n    \\\"test_3\\\": {\\\"functional\\\": true, \\\"aesthetic\\\": true, \\\"faithful\\\": true, \\\"notes\\\": \\\"...\\\"},\\n    \\\"test_4\\\": {\\\"functional\\\": true, \\\"aesthetic\\\": true, \\\"faithful\\\": true, \\\"notes\\\": \\\"...\\\"}\\n  },\\n  \\\"issues_found\\\": [],\\n  \\\"ready_for_next\\\": true\\n}\\n```\\n\"}","tool_call_id":"toolu_01RDaKpXhY4Fze7mk8QtSogP","id":null,"provider_name":null,"provider_details":null,"part_kind":"tool-call"}],"usage":{"input_tokens":48965,"cache_write_tokens":837,"cache_read_tokens":48127,"output_tokens":6269,"input_audio_tokens":0,"cache_audio_read_tokens":0,"output_audio_tokens":0,"details":{"cache_creation_input_tokens":837,"cache_read_input_tokens":48127,"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":6269}},"model_name":"claude-opus-4-5-20251101","timestamp":"2026-02-06T20:22:44.137195Z","kind":"response","provider_name":"anthropic","provider_url":"https://api.anthropic.com","provider_details":{"finish_reason":"tool_use","zo_persona_id":"74e0a70d-398a-4337-bcab-3e5a3a9d805c","zo_persona_name":"Vibe Architect"},"provider_response_id":"msg_016HHY3rU6vxQYGcJyEEzuER","finish_reason":"tool_call","run_id":"97a0d03b-2869-42c4-a37e-2bcbaa05238f","metadata":null}
